title: Tempting Yeasty Argentinian Cheese Empanadas
description: A kid-friendly Argentinian dish featuring green olives and gooey cheese, these empanadas are lacto-vegetarian-friendly and perfect for a snack. They are made with a yeasty dough and filled with a delicious mixture of cheese, olives, and spices.
instructions: In a large bowl, combine the flour, yeast, and salt.
Gradually add the agave nectar and olive oil to the dry ingredients and mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into 8 equal pieces and roll each piece into a ball.
Flatten each ball into a disk shape and place a tablespoon of cheese, a few olives, and a sprinkle of fry grind rice in the center of each disk.
Fold the dough over the filling and press the edges together to seal the empanada.
Place the empanadas on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and brush with olive oil.
Bake the empanadas for 15 minutes or until golden brown.
